raw_consumer_statement:
o Whom It May Concern, I am submitting this formal complaint against Barclays for their refusal to open a billing dispute regarding a charge of {$810.00} from XXXX XXXX, citing an arbitrary and unreasonable submission deadline of 10 days. This refusal is in direct violation of Regulation Z of the Truth in Lending Act ( TILA ) ( 12 CFR 1026.13 ), which grants consumers 60 days from the statement date to dispute a charge. 

Despite my multiple attempts to contact XXXX XXXX regarding this unauthorized or improper charge, the merchant has failed to respond or provide resolution. As my card issuer, Barclays has a legal obligation to investigate my dispute in good faith and can not impose policies that unlawfully restrict my rights under federal law. 

I demand that Barclays immediately open a dispute investigation into this charge and comply with its obligations under TILA. Their failure to do so constitutes unfair and deceptive practices under the Consumer Financial Protection Act ( 12 U.S.C. 5531 ). If this matter is not promptly addressed, I will have no choice but to escalate my complaint to the Office of the Comptroller of the Currency ( OCC ) and consider legal remedies available to me.

I expect a written response acknowledging this complaint and outlining the steps Barclays will take to resolve this matter. Please consider this my formal request for regulatory intervention.
